title: Add user to hyperv admin group during podman machine init
body:
### Feature request description

This came up when discussing with @l0rd about https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/24813

With that PR, it is possible to run podman on hyperv as a normal user (elevated rights are only asked for registry stuff). 
However, the constraint that we have at that point is that the user must be in the hyper-v admin group, otherwise they will not be able to create/start/stop/delete/... any VM.

### Suggest potential solution

During the initialization of a podman machine we should check if the user is already in the hyperv admin group and, if not, add it there.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

### Additional context

This issue is related to https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/24813 and i opened it so we don't lose this enhancement idea.
I'll close/update it based on how that PR evolves.

comments.body: For the OpenShift Local installer we add the current user to the Hyper-V Administrators group during installation itself (as it is usual to ask for Admin privilege during installation), maybe that could be an option here as well https://github.com/crc-org/crc/blob/231ecf758774c761deda73032402de4cd6ed6928/packaging/windows/product.wxs.template#L89

Thank you @anjannath for the suggestion. There are a couple of reasons for not adding the capability in the installer:
1) we recently modified the installer so that it doesn't require admin privileges (one of customers' most requested features)
2) with `podman machine init` capable to add the user to hyper-v admins, the problem get fixed no matter how Podman got installed (scoop, choco, winget, built from sources etc...)

Thanks for the quick reply, indeed adding it to `podman machine init` makes more sense to cover all the different installation scenarios 

Regarding this, will the user need to run `podman machine init` in a Powershell session with Administrator privileges, or `podman machine init` itself would do the privilege escalation and prompt user to accept the UAC?

I think the later is desirable, as otherwise user will have to run `podman machine init` and then open another Powershell window without Administrator privileges to run the other podman commands.

> I think the later is desirable, as otherwise user will have to run podman machine init and then open another Powershell window without Administrator privileges to run the other podman commands.

This is exactly what we want to do. See the [related issue](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/27627) and the [epic](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/25723).
